pattern: A complete binary tree has no gaps when written out level by level, so its level-order listing doubles as an implicit array: node i's children live at 2i+1 and 2i+2, and the next insertion slot is always just past the end of that array.
hints: A binary tree is given (and must be returned) as its level-order array with no null padding, because it is guaranteed complete: index 0 is the root, and node at index i has children at 2i+1 and 2i+2 when those indices exist.
Keep the tree as that flat array internally; appending a value to the end of the array is exactly the same as inserting the next node of a complete tree in level order.

insert(v) appends v, making its new index k the array length before the append; its parent's index is (k - 1) // 2 and that parent's value is what insert() must return. get_root() just hands back the whole array.

edge_inputs.description: single-node tree grows through two insertions
starting tree already has one child on the left only
minimum tree of one node, no insertions, get_root only
perfectly full two-level tree (3 nodes) about to start a brand new level
several consecutive inserts must each report the correct (changing) parent as the tree fills a level then starts a new one
starting from a perfectly full tree, the next two inserts become the left and right child of the same parent — both must report that same parent value

stress.description: start from a random complete tree of random size and insert many values in a row
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    start_n = rng.randint(1, 200)
    root = [rng.randint(0, 5000) for _ in range(start_n)]
    ops = ['CBTInserter']
    args = [[root]]
    inserts = max(1, size // 10)
    for _ in range(inserts):
        ops.append('insert')
        args.append([rng.randint(0, 5000)])
    ops.append('get_root')
    args.append([])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: root is supplied as a complete-tree level-order array (no nulls, since a complete tree of size n always fills indices 0..n-1)

complexity.time: O(1) per insert, O(n) for get_root
